recipes.title: Push Project to GitHub
recipes.description: Full wizard — git init, configure identity, SSH key setup, connect remote, commit, push.

steps.title: Check git is installed
steps.description_noob: First let me check git is installed on your device.
steps.description_learner: Verifying git installation before proceeding.

steps.title: Check git identity
steps.description_noob: Checking if git knows your name — it needs this before saving your work.
steps.description_learner: Verifying git user.name and user.email are configured.

steps.title: Configure git identity
steps.command: git config --global user.name "{name}" && git config --global user.email "{email}" && git config --global init.defaultBranch main
steps.description_noob: I need your name and email for git. Use the same email as your GitHub account.
steps.description_learner: Setting user.name, user.email, and default branch to main.

steps.title: Initialize git repo
steps.description_noob: Setting up this folder as a Git project.
steps.description_learner: Running git init if .git/ doesn't exist yet.

steps.title: Stage all files
steps.description_noob: Marking all your files to be saved.
steps.description_learner: Staging all changes with git add .

steps.title: Create first commit
steps.description_noob: Saving a snapshot of your project.
steps.description_learner: Creating the initial commit. --allow-empty handles empty repos.

steps.title: Check SSH key
steps.description_noob: Checking if you have an SSH key set up for GitHub.
steps.description_learner: Checking for ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

steps.title: Generate SSH key
steps.description_noob: Creating a secure key so GitHub can identify you without a password.
steps.description_learner: Generating ed25519 SSH key pair. -N '' = no passphrase for automation.

steps.title: Show your SSH public key
steps.description_noob:
Here is your SSH key. You need to add this to GitHub before continuing.

  Steps:
  1. Copy the key that appears below
  2. Go to github.com → Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key
  3. Paste it and save
  4. Come back here and press Enter to continue
steps.description_learner: Copy this public key and add it to GitHub Settings → SSH Keys.
steps.pause_message: Press Enter after you've added the SSH key to GitHub:

steps.title: Connect to GitHub
steps.command: git remote remove origin 2>/dev/null; git remote add origin git@github.com:{github_username}/{repo_name}.git
steps.description_noob: Connecting your project to GitHub. I need your GitHub username and repo name.
steps.description_learner: Setting the remote origin to your GitHub repo via SSH.

steps.title: Set branch to main
steps.description_noob: Setting the branch name to 'main' — the GitHub standard.
steps.description_learner: Renaming branch to main (GitHub default).

steps.title: Push to GitHub
steps.description_noob: Uploading your project to GitHub now!
steps.description_learner: Pushing with -u to set upstream tracking. After this, just git push works.

recipes.title: Setup Python Dev Environment
recipes.description: Install Python, pip, create a virtual environment, and set up a project structure.

steps.title: Install Python 3
steps.description_noob: Installing Python 3 — the programming language you'll be using.
steps.description_learner: Installing python3 via pkg. Includes pip automatically.

steps.title: Upgrade pip
steps.description_noob: Updating Python's package installer to the latest version.
steps.description_learner: Upgrading pip to latest. Avoids version warnings on install.

steps.title: Create project folder
steps.description_noob: Creating a folder for your project.
steps.description_learner: Creating project directory.

steps.title: Create virtual environment
steps.description_noob: Creating a private Python environment just for this project.
steps.description_learner: python3 -m venv creates isolated environment. Activate with source venv/bin/activate.

steps.title: Create main.py
steps.description_noob: Creating a starter Python file for your project.
steps.description_learner: Creating main.py entry point.

steps.title: Create requirements.txt
steps.description_noob: Creating a requirements file — this will list all the Python packages your project needs.
steps.description_learner: Creating empty requirements.txt. Add packages with pip install X then pip freeze > requirements.txt.

steps.title: Show how to activate
steps.description_noob:
Your Python project is ready!

  To start working:
  $ cd {project_name}
  $ source venv/bin/activate
  $ python3 main.py
steps.description_learner: Setup complete. cd {project_name} && source venv/bin/activate to start.

recipes.title: Download YouTube Video
recipes.description: Install yt-dlp and ffmpeg, then download a video to your Downloads folder.

steps.title: Install yt-dlp
steps.description_noob: Installing yt-dlp — the tool that downloads videos.
steps.description_learner: yt-dlp is the maintained fork of youtube-dl. Supports 1000+ sites.

steps.title: Install ffmpeg
steps.description_noob: Installing ffmpeg — needed to convert and process the video files.
steps.description_learner: ffmpeg is required by yt-dlp for audio/video merging and conversion.

steps.title: Check storage access
steps.description_noob: Checking if Termux can save to your Downloads folder.
steps.description_learner: Verifying ~/storage/downloads symlink exists.

steps.title: Setup storage access
steps.description_noob: Giving Termux permission to save to your Downloads folder. A popup will appear — tap Allow.
steps.description_learner: termux-setup-storage creates ~/storage symlinks. Accept the permission popup.
steps.pause_message: Press Enter after tapping Allow on the popup:

steps.title: Get video URL
steps.description_noob: What's the YouTube URL you want to download?
steps.description_learner: Enter the video URL.

steps.title: Download video
steps.description_noob: Downloading now — this may take a few minutes depending on your connection. The video will appear in your Downloads folder.
steps.description_learner: Downloading to ~/storage/downloads/ with title as filename.

recipes.title: Make Termux Look Good
recipes.description: Install zsh, Oh My Zsh, and a clean theme for a much better terminal experience.

steps.title: Install zsh
steps.description_noob: Installing zsh — a much better shell than the default one.
steps.description_learner: Installing zsh, curl, and git (all required for Oh My Zsh).

steps.title: Install Oh My Zsh
steps.description_noob: Installing Oh My Zsh — this gives you a beautiful, colorful terminal.
steps.description_learner: --unattended installs without prompts. Sets zsh as default shell automatically.

steps.title: Set theme to agnoster
steps.description_noob: Setting the 'agnoster' theme — a clean, informative look.
steps.description_learner: Replacing default robbyrussell theme with agnoster in ~/.zshrc.

steps.title: Add useful aliases
steps.command:
echo '
alias ll="ls -la"
alias gs="git status"
alias gp="git push"
alias gl="git log --oneline -10"' >> ~/.zshrc
steps.description_noob: Adding shortcuts: 'll' for list files, 'gs' for git status, 'gp' for git push.
steps.description_learner: Adding ll, gs, gp, gl aliases to ~/.zshrc.

steps.title: Switch to zsh
steps.description_noob: Making zsh your default shell. Restart Termux to see the new look!
steps.description_learner: Setting zsh as default shell. Takes effect on next Termux session.
steps.pause_message: Press Enter, then restart Termux to see your new shell:

recipes.title: Set Up Local Web Server
recipes.description: Start a local web server to serve files on your network.

steps.title: Check Python installed
steps.description_noob: Checking if Python is installed — we'll use it to run the server.
steps.description_learner: Verifying python3 is available.

steps.title: Check storage access
steps.description_noob: Making sure Termux can access your files.
steps.description_learner: Checking storage permission.

steps.title: Choose port
steps.description_noob: Which port should the server use? Press Enter for the default (8080).
steps.description_learner: Port for the HTTP server. 8080 is safe. 80 requires root.

steps.title: Get your IP address
steps.command: ip addr show | grep 'inet ' | grep -v '127.0.0.1' | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d/ -f1 | head -1
steps.description_noob: Finding your phone's IP address so you can open the server in a browser.
steps.description_learner: Showing local IP address for LAN access.

steps.title: Start the server
steps.description_noob:
Server is starting! Open your browser and go to: http://YOUR_IP:{port}
  Press Ctrl+C when you want to stop the server.
steps.description_learner: python3 -m http.server serves current directory. --bind 0.0.0.0 allows LAN access.

recipes.title: Backup Termux to SD Card
recipes.description: Create a complete backup of your Termux home and packages to your Downloads folder.

steps.title: Check storage access
steps.description_noob: Checking if Termux can write to your Downloads folder.
steps.description_learner: Verifying /sdcard/Download is accessible.

steps.title: Setup storage access
steps.description_noob: Setting up storage access first. Tap Allow on the popup.
steps.description_learner: termux-setup-storage needed before backup.
steps.pause_message: Press Enter after allowing storage access:

steps.title: Check available space
steps.description_noob: Checking if your SD card has enough space for the backup.
steps.description_learner: Checking /sdcard free space before backup.

steps.title: Create backup archive
steps.description_noob: Creating your backup now. This includes all your files and installed packages. May take a minute.
steps.description_learner: Archiving Termux home and usr directories. Date-stamped filename.

steps.title: Show backup location
steps.description_noob: Your backup is saved in your Downloads folder. You can find it in the Files app.
steps.description_learner: Showing the created backup file size and location.

recipes.title: Setup Node.js Project
recipes.description: Install Node.js, initialize a project, install Express, and create a starter server.

steps.title: Install Node.js
steps.description_noob: Installing Node.js — the JavaScript runtime for your server.
steps.description_learner: Installing nodejs via pkg. Includes npm automatically.

steps.title: Create project folder
steps.description_noob: Creating your project folder.
steps.description_learner: Creating project directory.

steps.title: Initialize npm project
steps.description_noob: Setting up your Node.js project — creates a package.json file.
steps.description_learner: npm init -y creates package.json with defaults.

steps.title: Install Express
steps.description_noob: Installing Express — the most popular web framework for Node.js.
steps.description_learner: npm install express adds Express to node_modules and package.json.

steps.title: Create starter server
steps.description_noob: Creating a starter Express server file.
steps.description_learner: Creating index.js with basic Express server on port 3000.

steps.title: How to start
steps.description_noob:
Your Node.js project is ready!

  To start the server:
  $ cd {project_name}
  $ node index.js

  Then open: http://localhost:3000
steps.description_learner: Run: cd {project_name} && node index.js. Open http://localhost:3000.

recipes.title: Compress and Share Folder
recipes.description: Zip a folder and share it via Android share sheet.

steps.title: Check zip installed
steps.description_noob: Checking zip is available.
steps.description_learner: Verifying zip command.

steps.title: Choose folder to compress
steps.description_noob: Here are the files in your current folder. Which one do you want to compress?
steps.description_learner: Showing current directory contents.

steps.title: Create zip archive
steps.description_noob: Compressing your folder into a zip file.
steps.description_learner: zip -r creates a recursive archive of the folder.

steps.title: Show archive size
steps.description_noob: Here's your compressed file and its size.
steps.description_learner: Showing compressed archive size.

steps.title: Share the file
steps.description_noob: Opening the share menu so you can send it to WhatsApp, Drive, email, etc.
steps.description_learner: termux-share opens Android intent chooser. Needs termux-api.

recipes.title: Fix Termux Storage Access
recipes.description: Diagnose and fix all common Termux storage permission issues.

steps.title: Check current storage state
steps.description_noob: Checking what storage access you currently have.
steps.description_learner: Checking ~/storage symlinks exist.

steps.title: Run storage setup
steps.description_noob:
Running the storage setup. A popup will appear — tap Allow.

  If you've done this before but storage still doesn't work, tap Allow again — it resets the permission.
steps.description_learner: termux-setup-storage requests permission and creates ~/storage symlinks.
steps.pause_message: Press Enter after tapping Allow on the popup:

steps.title: Verify storage works
steps.description_noob: Checking if storage access is working now.
steps.description_learner: Verifying ~/storage/downloads is accessible.

steps.title: Test write access
steps.description_noob: Testing that you can actually save files to your Downloads folder.
steps.description_learner: Write test: create and delete a test file in Downloads.

recipes.title: Update Everything
recipes.description: Update Termux packages, Python packages, and npm global packages.

steps.title: Update package list
steps.description_noob: Refreshing the list of available packages.
steps.description_learner: pkg update fetches latest package index from Termux repos.

steps.title: Upgrade all packages
steps.description_noob: Installing newer versions of all your tools. This may take a few minutes.
steps.description_learner: pkg upgrade installs newer versions of all installed packages.

steps.title: Upgrade Python packages
steps.command: pip list --outdated --format=columns 2>/dev/null | tail -n +3 | awk '{print $1}' | xargs -r pip install --upgrade 2>/dev/null || echo 'No Python packages to update'
steps.description_noob: Updating all your Python packages.
steps.description_learner: Upgrading all outdated pip packages.

steps.title: Upgrade npm global packages
steps.command: npm update -g 2>/dev/null || echo 'No global npm packages'
steps.description_noob: Updating global Node.js tools.
steps.description_learner: npm update -g upgrades all globally installed npm packages.

steps.title: Clean up old files
steps.description_noob: Removing old downloaded package files to free up space.
steps.description_learner: pkg autoclean removes cached .deb files.
